publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday May 18, 2026 at 22:25

did.unittitle: Patent medicine and pharmacies collection

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

did.abstract: The collection includes patent medicine advertising and product labels, advertising for quack medicine health products using magnetism and electricity, two scrapbooks containing patent medicine labels and trade cards, and materials related to pharmacies in Connecticut and Rhode Island. Types of advertising materials include single sheets, brochures, pamphlets, trade cards, and matchbook covers. Product labels include a wide variety of patent medicine products and labels produced for the G. E. Greene Pharmacy in Hope Valley, Rhode Island.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The collection was created by Medical Historical Library staff from materials gifted by William Helfand, 1985, with addtional materials gifted by Bert Hansen, 2019-2022 (item description may include a Hansen database number), and Oliver Bristol Bailey, 2023.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized in five series: Series I. Patent medicine advertisements, 1827-1990. Series II. Medical devices and health systems, circa 1880-1919. Series III. Patent medicine labels, circa 1867-1924, undated. Series IV. Patent medicine labels and trade cards scrapbooks, circa 1845-1882. Series V. Pharmacies, 1848-1966.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Patent medicine and pharmacies collection. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: For related materials see Pam Coll 3, Patent medicine almanacs, and Pam Coll 16, Pharmaceutical and chemical company publications.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes patent medicine advertisements and labels; advertisements for quack medicine devices; and materials from pharmacies in Connecticut and Rhode Island. Patent medicines were non-prescription, pre-packaged compounds sold under a proprietary name in the 1800s and early 1900s. Manufacturers sold these products directly to customers, either in person or by mail order, or by marketing to pharmacies. Patent medicine marketing used claims of curing wide varieties of illnesses and/or conditions and colorful imagery, some of which included racist or offensive ethnic stereotypes. Prior to the passing of the 1906 Pure Food and Drug Act, contents of patent medicines were only partially disclosed. Labels and marketing might mention herbal ingredients, but tended to omit pain relievers or mood enhancing drugs such as alcohol, cannabis, chloroform, cocaine, heroin, laudanum, or opium.

Series I. Patent medicine advertisements, 1827-1990, includes single sheets, brochures, pamphlets, trade cards, matchbook covers, and newspapers advertising patent medicines produced in the United States, England, and France. Advertisements for French products were removed from issues of Le Progrès Médical , "journal de médicine, de chirurgie et de pharmacie," a publication of the Union Therapeutique Internationale.

Series II. Medical devices and health systems, circa 1880-1919, includes materials advertising quack medicine devices and health diet regiments. Materials include promotions for magnetic healing, "electric" and 'electro-magnetic" appliances and garments, a vacuum pump, and rectal dilators.

Series III. Patent medicine labels, circa 1867-1924, includes unused product labels created for manufacturers of a variety of patent medicines, product labels created for G. E. Greene Pharmacy in Rhode Island, and marketing materials from companies selling box and bottle labels.

Series IV. Patent medicine labels and trade cards scrapbooks, circa 1845-1882, includes two scrapbooks. The scrapbook titled "Drug Labels" includes product labels created for numerous pharmacies in Rhode Island and Connecticut and several receipts and checks from the Stonington Bank for goods and shipment of goods to Stonington, Connecticut dating 1860-1882. The Jennie Bristol Stevens album includes trade cards for numerous patent medicines, products, and businesses in the New Haven area. Several trade cards in this scrapbook depict Black people using racist stereotypes.

Series V. Pharmacies, 1848-1966, includes product order slips, invoices, freight receipts, and prescription slips from pharmacies in Connecticut and Rhode Island. This material was created during the business operations of one or more of the following pharmacies: E. G. Champlin and Company in Westerly, Rhode Island; J. H. Potter, Druggist and Apothecary, Westerly, Rhode Island; Potter and Champlin, Druggists, Westerly, Rhode Island; Greene and Champlin, Wyoming, Rhode Island; and G. E. Greene, Hope Valley, Rhode Island; H. O. Williams, Druggist in Old Mystic, Connecticut; and C. E. Brayton and Company in Stonington, Connecticut. The series also includes ephemera related to pharmacies including a photograph of G. E. Greene in his pharmacy, a coloring book, a train schedule with advertisements, and a vinegar valentine titled "The Druggist."

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners.

did.unittitle: Patent medicine advertisements
A. J. White, Limited, London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet A guide to palmistry by Zillah , includes 1902 calendar and advertisements for Siegel's Syrup, Mother Siegel's Ointment, Mother Siegel's operating Pills, and Laxol.

did.unittitle: A. L. Scovill Company, New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet Annual family receipt book and useful medical adviser or every body's book with advertisements for Dr. A. Rogers' Compound Syrup of liverwort, tar, and conchalagua and Mexican Mustang Liniment.

did.unittitle: Alta Pharmacal Company, St. Louis, Missouri

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advertisement from unidenfied issue of the Medical Record for four Alta Pharmacal Company products: Rheumagon, "a normal osmotic"; Phaselin Tablets for "digestive, nutritive, absorptive, stimulant, aseptic"; Phaselin Powder "for local lesions and all kinds of wounds"; and Melachol, "an ideal nerve tonic, laxative, analgesic..."

did.unittitle: Antikamnia Chemical Company, St. Louis, Missouri

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Antikamnia tablets calendar for 1906, image on reverse of child nuns holding antikamnia tablets, with slogan "Opposed to Pain."

did.unittitle: Battle and Company Chemists' Corporation, St. Louis, Missouri

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Forceps deliveries and versions pamphlet with advertisements for Bromidia, Papine, Ecthol, and Iodia.

did.unittitle: Brown Chemical Company, Baltimore, Maryland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet A.B.C.s of home knowledge, amusement for the little ones with 1882 calendar on back cover and advertisements for Brown's Bitters, Curantine, and Dr. Mettaur's Headache Pills.

did.unittitle: Burkhart Medical Company, Cincinnati, Ohio

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Matchbox cover advertising Burkhart's System Builder, a tonic for "indigestion, torpid liver, and constipation."

did.unittitle: C. Henry Wilson, M.D., Brooklyn, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet containing patient testimonials promoting Cancerine "for the treatment of cancers, tumors, lupus, and all other forms of malignant blood diseases and benign growths." Hansen #3328.

did.unittitle: C. I. Hood and Company, Lowell, Massachusetts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five pamphlets and brochures advertising multiple Hood's products including Hood's Sarsparilla, Olive Ointment, Tooth Powder, and Medicated Soap and TusSano Pain-Killing Plasters. Pamphlet themes include home made candies, a cook book, and parlor games.

did.unittitle: Charles A. Voegler Company, Baltimore, Maryland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Second and third annual St. Jacobs Oil Family Calendar and book of health and humor . Pamphlets include advertisements for numerous Voegler Company products including St. Jacob's Oil, Dr. August Koenig's Hamburg Drops, and Dr. August Koenig's Breast Tea,

did.unittitle: Charles H. Church, Apothecary, New Bedford, Massachussets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Small pamphlet titled Handy housekeeper's companion with recipes for food and promotions for products sold by Charles H. Church, Apothecary. Products include Improved Liver Regulator, Hystoria, Celery and Chamomile Bitters, Eau Dentale, Saponaceous Tooth Powder,Brazilian Insect Powder, Eclectic Liniment, and Corn Cure among others.

did.unittitle: Chattanooga Medicine Company, Tennessee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet The Cardui song book with advertisement for McElree's Wine of Cardui and Cardui Wash for women's health and Thedford's Black-draught for the liver.

did.unittitle: D. B. Dewey and Company, New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Centaur Receipt Book advertising Ash-Tonic, Centaur Liniment, Castoria, and Dr. Wei De Meyer's Catarrh Cure.

did.unittitle: D. Ransom, Son and Company, Buffalo, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet titled Ransom's 1924 1925 family receipt book advertising multiple products including Ransom's Hive Syrup and Tolu, Trask's Ointment, Trask's Ointment Iodized, Miller's Universal Balm, Cooper's Universal Balm, Anderson's Dermador, and Dermador Ocular.

did.unittitle: Donald Kennedy, Roxbury, Massachusetts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet Diseases of the skin advertising Kennedy's plant based cure for skin diseases. Product name is not mentioned.

did.unittitle: Dr. B. J. Kendall and Company, Enosburgh Falls, Vermont

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two pamphlets: Treatise on disease , advertising Dr. B. J. Kendall's Tonic and Blood Purifier, Pectorial Elixir, and Blackberry Balsam, and The doctor at home , advertising Kendall's Persion Pills, which includes health care information for humans and horses.

did.unittitle: Dr. J. C. Ayer and Company, Lowell, Massachussetts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ayer's book of emergencies pamphlet promoting Ayer's Sarsparilla.

did.unittitle: Dr. J. H. Moseley, Brooklyn, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet The family handbook with advertisements for numerous products including Life Root Pills for menstrual problems; Leucopillule Pills for prolapsed uterus; Rheumatic Pills; Aural OIl for deafness; Pile Electuary, Atkinson's Golden Ointment for skin irritations; and Irritating Plaster and Compound Mandrake Pills to treat a large variety of conditions. Includes four pages of text in German.

did.unittitle: Dr. J. H. Schenck and Son, Philadelphia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet Family hand-book guide containing general health and emergency health advice and advertisements for Schenck's Pulmonic Syrup and Mandrake Liver Pills,

did.unittitle: Dr. John B. Houche, Groton, Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flier advrtising Dr. Houche's Bilious Pills.

did.unittitle: Dr. Kilmer and Company, Binghamton, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet advertising Dr. Kilmer's Swamp-Root for kidney health and seven cards presented to a druggist in Stonington, Connecticut for free bottles of Swamp-Root.

did.unittitle: Dr. Richards Dyspepsia Tablet Association, New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sheet of six different preprinted testimonial forms for Dr. Richard's Dyspepsia Tablets wiuth blanks for names of stores to be entered and and a self addresed envelope. Gift of H.A. Farr, May 1940.

did.unittitle: Dr. Shoop Family Medicine Company, Racine, Wisconsin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet advertising Dr. Shoop's Restorative that "strengthens the inside nerves" and can be used to treat stomach, liver, and kidney diseases and diabetes and Dr. Shoop's NIght Cure, for women.

did.unittitle: Dr. Taft Brothers, Rochester, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet advertising Dr. Taft's family medicines including Asthmalene, Brain and Nerve Bitters, Sure Relief, White Pine Syrup, and Balm of Gilead.

did.unittitle: Dr. Thomas W. Graydon, Cincinnati, Ohio

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two pamphlets advertising the Andral Broca cure for consumption, which includes medication and an inhalation device, and an addressed envelope.

did.unittitle: E. C. DeWitt and Company Incorporated, Chicago

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: DeWitt's 200 Year Calendar and Book of Horoscopes promoting multiple DeWitt's products, including DeWitt'a Pills, "a diuretic stimulant for the kidneys"; DeWitt's Antacid Powder; DeWitt's Nutos for muascular pain; DeWitt's Hygienic Powder for women; and Anzana "for rectal Irritations." Includes coupon's for free samples.

did.unittitle: Emerson and Company, Baltimore, Maryland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Small notebook advertising Bromo-Seltzer in English, French, Italian, and Yiddish. Includes offer for free sheet music.

did.unittitle: F and F Laboratories, Chicago

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two matchbook covers advertising F and F Lozenges for soothing the throat, freshening the breath, and "adding pleasure to smoking."

did.unittitle: G. and G. Stern, London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pages from an unknown publication advertising Pepsalia, the digestive table salt.

did.unittitle: G. C. Manford Manufacturing Company, Syracuse, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet ABC Jingles promoting Hanford's Balsam Myrrh for use on external injuries, on humans and horses. Hansen gift.

did.unittitle: G. G. Green, Woodbury, New Jersey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of pamphlet Wit and wisdom advertising Green's August Flower, Boschee's German Syrup, and Green's Ague Conqueror.

did.unittitle: G. M. Sands Company, Griffithville, Arkansas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box label for Tubercorem, an inhalant treatment for tuberculosis.

did.unittitle: George Wiliam Reed Bitter Company, New Haven, Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Trade card for Reed's Gilt Edge Tonic for malaria and indigestion. Front of card includes an ilustration of a woman with the caption "The late Miss Jennie E. Cramer, New Haven, Conn. Found dead on the beach at West Haven Conn. Aug. 5th 1881, supposed to have ben murdered with a poem on the death of Jennie E. Cramer." Back of card is a poem "The Death of Jennie E. Cramer" printed in the New Haven Union, August 24, 1881.

did.unittitle: Graefenberg Company, New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advertisement on page 3 of newspaper The Western World , New York, March 1848. Advertisement fills one full and three partial columns, includes advertising for multiple products and a letter of endorsement from William Brady, Mayor of New York. Gift of H. A. Farr, May, 1940. Gift of Bert Hansen.

did.unittitle: H. Browning Talyor, LeRaysville, Pennsylvania

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flier advertising "Taylor's celebrated condition powders for horses" on one side and multiple Taylor products on the reverse side including Taylor's Celebrated Oil, German Tincture, Cough Syrup, Indian Vegetable Sticking Salve, Aromatic Tooth Powders, Hair Tonic, and Anti-Billious Vegetable Pills.

did.unittitle: H. E. Bucklen and Company, Chicago and Windsor, Ontario

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two pamphlets, Dr. King's Guide to Health, Household Instructor, and Family Prize Cookbook and Dr. King's Lucky Book , both promoting Dr. King's New Discovery for Coughs, Colds, and all Bronchial Affections.

did.unittitle: H. H. Warner and Company, Rochester, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two pamphlets titled Book of $2000.00, prize enigmas and Health insures beauty promoting Warner's Safe Liver and Kidney Cure and Warner's Safe Yeast.

did.unittitle: H. R. Stevens, Boston

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet The national Life, containing biographical sketches of the president osf the United States from Wahington to Hayes... advertising Vegetine, "nature's remedy, the great blood purifier."

did.unittitle: Hazeltine and Company (Piso Company), Warren, Pennsylvania

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlets titled Advertising for a wife , The factory girl , and Piso's nursery rhymes promoting multiple products including Piso's Cure for Consumption, Piso's Remedy, Catarrh Balm, Tablets, and Antiseptic Tooth Powder. Also a matchbook cover advertising Piso's Cough Medicine, a version of Piso's Cure for Consumption created after 1907.

did.unittitle: Henry, Johnson and Lord, Burlington, Vermont

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Household receipt book for 1876 advertising products including Shaker Anodyne, Dr. H. Baxter's Anti-bilious and Jaundice Mandrake Bitters, Compound Arnica and Oil LInament, Vegetable Worm Lozenges, and Woodruff's Chain-Lightning Fly-Paper.

did.unittitle: J. E. Jackson, Woodbury, New Jersey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brochure promoting Dr. L. Burdick's Kidney Cure and Magnum Bonum cough syrup.

did.unittitle: J. P. Dinsmore, New York, and Seth W. Fowle and Sons, Boston

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet titled Songs for the little ones on one side and New rhymes for the nursery on the other side. Includes advertisements for Peruvian Syrup, "an iron tonic for the blood," and Wistar's Balsam of Wild Cherry for lung problems.

did.unittitle: J. T. Davenport, London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advertisement for Dr. J. Collis Browne's Chlorodyne.

did.unittitle: Jeremiah Curtis and Sons, New York, and John I. Brown and Sons, Boston

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mrs. Winslow Receipt Books for 1869-1874 advertising multiple products: Mrs. Winslow's Soothing Syrup, Brown's Bronchial Troches, Brown's Vermifuge Comfits, and the Household Panacea and Family Linament. 1869-1873 are stitched together.

did.unittitle: John B. Marchisi and Company, Utica, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet advertising Dr. Marchisi's Uterine Catholicon for women's diseases.

did.unittitle: John F. Henry, Curran and Company/Manhattan Medicine Company, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of pamphlet The phunny book advertising multiple products including Dr. Roger's Compound Syrup of liverwort, tar, and canchalagua; Egyptian Rat and Vermin Destroyer; Phalon's Vitalia hair tonic; Packer's Tar Soap, Holloway's Pills, Holloway's Ointment; and many more. John F. Henry, Curran and Company was founded in 1873 and changed its name to Manhattan Medicine Company in 1875.

did.unittitle: Joseph Keller, New York City, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Trade card promoting Keller's Sure Cure for Diptheria and Croup with illustration of a child holding a bottle of medication on one side, text on the other side. Hansen #5371.

did.unittitle: Kikapoo Indian Medicine Company, New Haven, Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five items: four pamphlets, including Life and scenes among the Kickapoo Indians , Kickapoo doctor , The Kickapoo's fortune-telling book , and Health and happiness ; and a brochure for Indian Worm KIller. Products advertised include Indian Sagwa, Indian Oil, Kikapoo Salve and Indian Worm Killer. Previous locations of the Kickapoo Indian Medicine Company include Athol and Boston, Massachusetts and Providence, Rhode Island.

did.unittitle: Kikapoo Indian Medicine Company, New Haven, Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 16 page newspaper The Red Man . Products advertised include Indian Sagwa, Indian Oil, Kikapoo Salve and Indian Worm Killer. Previous locations of the Kickapoo Indian Medicine Company include Athol and Boston, Massachusetts and Providence, Rhode Island.

did.unittitle: Knoll and Company, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A pamphlet advertising Diuretin Knoll for treating kidney and vascular ailments and a brochure advertising Anthrasol, purified colorless tar, for treatment of skin diseases.

did.unittitle: L. Avery, M.D.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advertisement for cholera drops.

did.unittitle: Lewis Merriam, Greenfield, Massachusetts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet with religious theme advertising L. Merriam's Quaker Cough Cure and Quaker Sleep Promoter; Wilsonia Magnetic Appliances including sleeping cap, body belts (lady's and gent's), and "lung invigorator" apron; and Edison's Anchor Electro-Galvanic Belt. Includes excerpts from the Bible, moral and health advice, and hymns. Pamphlet cover is missing.

did.unittitle: Lydia E. Pinkham Medicine Company, Lynn, Massachusetts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Six pamphlets titled: How Phyllis grew thin , Our wild neighbors , First aid , Three hundred brain teasers , Needle-art and health book , and Picnic time , advertising Lydia E. Pinkham products including Vegetable Compound, Tablets, Herb Medicine, Pills for Constipation, and Sanative Wash.

did.unittitle: Lyon's Manufacturing Company, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two brochures titled Easy pictures for slate drawing advertising Sea Moss Farine, a nutritional supplement, Lyon's Kathairon, for the hair, Mexican Mustang Liniment, for use on "man and beast", Hagan's Magnolia Balm, for the complection, Vaughn's Lithontripic Mixture for treatment of multiple condition; and Plantation Bitters, for treating multiple symptoms, including women's diseases.

did.unittitle: M. J. Brietenbach Company, New York City, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Physician's daily memorandum book for 1901 promoting Gude's Pepto-Mangan to treat anemia. Owned by unnamed female medical professional (same owner as following entry) living in Hartford, Connecticut. Contains personal and medical diary entries, including one from September 14, 1901 about the assassination of President William McKinley. Hansen gift.

did.unittitle: M. J. Brietenbach Company, New York City, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Physician's daily memorandum book for 1906 promoting Gude's Pepto-Mangan to treat anemia. Owned by unnamed female medical professional (same owner as previous entry) living in Hartford, Connecticut. Contains personal and medical diary entries, Hansen gift,

did.unittitle: Magic Leaf Pill Company, Indianapolis, Indiana

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flier advertising Magic Leaf Pills, the "great blood purifyer (sic) and system tonic."

did.unittitle: Merchant's Gargling Oil Company, Lockport, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two pamphlets advertising Merchant's Gargling Oil Liniment; one from 1861 titled Valuable Recipes of General Usefulness ; the other from 1887 titled Merchant's Gargling Oil Songster , with a calendar and song lyrics.

did.unittitle: Merck and Company, New York and Rahway, New Jersey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five pamphlets advertising Ichthyol, a soluble sulphur compound; Digitan, a "physiologically standardized preparation of digitalis"; Arsenoferratose, an iron supplement with arsenic; and Iodine Vermicide, a deworming treatment being marketed for use by poultry farmers.

did.unittitle: Miles Laboratories, Elkhart, Indiana

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two pamphlets Our presidents and Hints for housekeepers advertising products from Dr. Miles Laboratories including Alka-Seltzer, Nervine, Aspir-Mint, Anti-Pain Pills, Laxative Tablets, and Cactus Compound.

did.unittitle: Monticello Drug Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two paper fans promoting 666 liquid, tablets, salve, and nose drops for the treatment of malaria, colds, headaches, neuralgia, and minors burns, cuts, and sores. Ilustration on front shows a baby sitting on a branch with three birds singing "666 Carol"; other side includes text promoting 666 products.

did.unittitle: Monticello Drug Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A paper fan promoting 666 liquid, tablets, salve, and nose drops for the treatment of malaria, colds, headaches, neuralgia, and minors burns, cuts, and sores. Front of fan includes illustration of the goddess Diana with a dog and a paragraph of text about her; other side includes text about 666 products

did.unittitle: O. Noble and Company, Erie, Pennsylvania

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet The housekeeper's receipt book advertising Carter's products including Compound Extract of Smart Weed, Nerve, Liver and Anti-Costive Pills, Extract Dandelion and Bitter Sweet, Balm of Gilead, Female Restorative, Ring Bone and Spavin Lotion.

did.unittitle: Pabst Brewing Company, Milwaukee, Wisconsin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet More secrets advertising Pabst Brewing Company's Extract of Malt and Hops. Includes humorous content, currency value chart, information about weights and measures, and testimonials.

did.unittitle: Palisade Manufacturing Company, Yonkers, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of pamphlet The works of that famous Chirurgion Ambrose Parey promoting Hemaboloids for the treatment of anemia. Hansen #3069.

did.unittitle: Pepsin Syrup Company, Monticello, Illinois

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet Dr. Caldwell's guide to health, home cook book advertising Dr. Caldwell's Syrup Pepsin for headache, stomach and bowel troubles. Includes a coupon for a free trial bottle

did.unittitle: Perry Davis and Son, Providence, Rhode Island

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two pamphlets advertising The Pain Killer and Allen's Lung Balsam

did.unittitle: Pneumo-Phthysine Chemical Manufacturing Company, Chicago, Illinois

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet promoting use of Pneumo-Phthysine as a fever reducer. Hansen gift.

did.unittitle: Pond's Extract Company, New York and London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet advertising Pond's Extract for internal and external use in the treatment of numerous conditions.

did.unittitle: R. T. Booth Company, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet promoting Booth's Hyomei "Dri-Ayr" pocket inhaler cure for catarhh, asthma, bronchitis, hay fever, etc.

did.unittitle: Redding and Company, Boston

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet advertising Doctor Sherman's All Healing Balsam, Tooth Paste, Poor Man's Plaster, Tooth Ache Drops, and cough lozenges.

did.unittitle: Rio Chemical Company, St. Louis, Missouri

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Large pamphlet titled Gallery of pictures of interest to medical men with color reproductions of paintings alternating with testimonials for Rio Chemical Company products Celerina, a "nerve tonic, stimulant, and antispasmodic"; Pinus Canadensis, a "non-irritating mucous astringent", and Aletris Cordial, a "uterine tonic and restorative." Hansen #5334

did.unittitle: Robert W. Gardner, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet Phosphorus, iodine and iron, physiologically considered advertising Gardner's Special Pharmaceutical Preparations.

did.unittitle: Viavi Company, New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet advertising Viavi, a treatment for women's health issues.

did.unittitle: W. H. Hyatt, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brochure promoting Hyatt's Infallible Life Balsam made of ingredients including sarsparilla, dock, and guaiacum.

did.unittitle: W. M. S. Merrell Company, Cincinnati, Ohio

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brochure for Infa-Concemin, a vitamin B complex with ferrous sulfate for children.

did.unittitle: Wells, Richardson and Company, Burlington, Vermont

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three copies of pamphlet titled Successful Home Dyeing with advertiesments for Diamond Dyes and Lactated Foods baby food.

did.unittitle: Wells, Richardson and Company, Burlington, Vermont

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a large pamphlet advertising Paine's Celery Compound, a pamphlet titled The Handy Cash Book with calendar for 1896, and three copies of The Handy Cash Book with calendar for 1897, also with advertisements for Paine's Celery Compound.

did.unittitle: White and Howland, New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet titled The household companion and family recipe book with advertisements for Moffat's Phoenix Bitters, Moffat's Life Pills, Page's Climax Salve, and Edey's Carbolic Troches.

did.unittitle: William Franklin and Company, New Haven, Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Circular for Myer's Compound Extract of Rock Rose, includes testimonials, many from New Haven, Connecticut. Circular was mailed to the postmaster in Southport, Connecticut.

did.unittitle: William R. Warner and Company, Philadelphia, New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brochure listing products and prices for multiple types of sugar coated pills, medicinal fluid extracts, and "new remedies, elixirs, and pharmaceutical products" a pamphlet titled Parvules: the superiority of small and frequent doses in hte treatment of disease with advertisements for Phosphorus Pills, Salicyclate of Soda with Bromide of Potash, Pepsin, Bismuth and Strychnine, Quinine Pills, and Brormo Soda; and a brocure titled Dyscheza , with an advertisement for Agarol Mineral Oil.

did.unittitle: William Radam Microbe Killer Company, New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet advertising W M. (William) Radam's Microbe Killer for use in treatment of multiple conditions.

did.unittitle: Williams and Company, Philadelphia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet titled Dr. Wistar's family medical guide; a treatise on consumption of the lungs.. . advertisng Wistar's Balsam of Wild Cherry.

did.unittitle: Winkelmann and Brown Drug Company, Baltimore, Maryland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet promoting multiple products including Pruneline, "the ideal laxative"; Kopfaline, "the great German headache cure'; Mortimer and Mowbray's Hampton Vegetable Tincture; and Larrabee's Rheumatic Liniment or Pain Extractor.

did.unittitle: World's Dispensary Medical Association, Buffalo, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three copies of Pierce's Memorandum and Account Book, designed for farmers, mechanics, and all people from 1885, 1900, and 1913 advertising Dr. Pierce's Golden Medical Discovery, Salve, Lotion Tablets, Suppositories, and Cough Syrup.

did.unittitle: British Almanac Companion advertising sheet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pages removed from the 1886 British Almanac Companion with advertisements for Norton's Camomile Pills, Godfrey's Extract of Elder flowers, and Steedman's Soothing Powders.

title.part: Bulletin Officiel du College des Medecins de l'Agglomeration Bruxellois

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete issue with advertisements for several patent medicines sold by Pharmacie Derneville in Brussells. Products include Lecithine Vial, Gustave Chanteaud Lentilles d'Alcaloides, oxygen, various serums, and first aid supplies.

title.part: Annales de l'Institute Chirugical de Bruxelles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete issue with advertisements for several patent medicines sold by Pharmacie Derneville in Brussells. Products include Serum Anticoquelucheux, Coton Iode, Ouataplasme, Carmeine, Sapolan, Elixir Maiche, and Pinel Malt Phosphate. Gift of Bert Hansen, 2022.

did.unittitle: Helfand Collection postcards, Philadelphia Museum of Art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two postcards with reproductions of advertisements for Bile Beans and No-To-Bac, part of the William H. Helfand Collection at the Philadelphia Museum of Art.

did.unittitle: Patent medicine advertisements from Le Progrès Médical

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Le Progrès Médical : Journal de médicine, de chirurgie et de pharmacie, was a publication of the Union Therapeutique Internationale, Paris, Librarie A. Duval. Materials in French.

did.unittitle: Deschiens, Paris

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advertisement for Sirop de Deschiens for treatment of anemia showing increase in the number of red blood cells after use of the product.

did.unittitle: J. B. Fialip, Paris

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advertisement for Dr. Roussel's Hemostyle "fresh from the horse" for treatment of anemia and hemmorhage and a postcard card to mail in for a free sample.

did.unittitle: Laboratoire F. Boudin, Vincennes, France

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advertisement with detachable postcard for Révulsif Boudin, a fast acting topical treatment for congestion.

did.unittitle: Laboratoire G. Fermé, Paris

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brochure for Triradol, a blend of menthol, camphor, and "radioactive elements" for treatment of multiple conditions. The back of the brocure lists additional products Muthanol, Neolyse et Neolyse Radioactive, Staphylothanol, and Visco-Serum.

did.unittitle: Laboratoire Zizine, Paris

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advertisement for three products: Agocholine, Sedogastrine, and Peptodiase and a postcard that may be mailed in for a free sample of one product.

did.unittitle: Laboratoires Charvoz, Lyon, France

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A brochure and a one page advertisement with detachable postcard for two products: Hepatogenes Charvoz, for liver problems, and Cachet Charvoz, for digestive problems.

did.unittitle: Laboratoires Laleuf,Paris

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two small brochures, one advertising Iodatoxine, a combination of iodine, caffeine, and nitrites, for treatment of heart and respiratory conditions; one advertising Trinitrine Caféinée, a combination of nitroglycerin, caffeine, and heroin, for treating heart conditions.

did.unittitle: Laboratoires de l'Opocalcium

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advertisement with detachable postcard for Opocalcium and Opocalcium Arsenie (with arsenic) to treat multiple conditions including tuberculosis, overwork, neurasthenia, fractures, etc. Includes dosage for children and adults.

did.unittitle: Medical devices and health systems
American School of Magnetic Healing, Nevada, Missouri

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet titled The profession of magnetic healing by Professor S. A. Weltmer.

did.unittitle: Electro-Magnetic Company, Chicago

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Small pamphlet titled T he guide to good luck advertising the Electro-Magnetic Pad and the Electr-Magnetic Plaster for treatment of rheumatism and "all diseases of the kidneys, spine, chest."

did.unittitle: Erie Medical Company, Buffalo, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes pamphlet, brochure of testimonials, order form with health questionnaire, and instructions for payment for vacuum appliance purported to restore the sexual health of men. Hansen gift.

did.unittitle: F. E. Young and Company, Chicago

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two pamphlets titled Dr. Young's radical cure and Dr. Young's radical remedy advertising Dr. Young's Ideal Rectal Dilators and Pile Ointment for treatment of constipation and piles.

did.unittitle: G. H. Brinkler, Washington D.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet featuring article "The philosophy of Leppelism" and promoting Brinkler's "Brainy Diet System."

did.unittitle: L. A. Bosworth, Boston, Massachusetts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of pamphlet promoting the Electropoise device for electroliberation treatment, "disease cured without drugs or medicine." Product manufactured by The Electrocalibration Company, Birmingham, Alabama.

did.unittitle: Professor Andrew Chrystal, Marshall, Michigan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet promoting Professor Chrystal's Electric Belts and Appliances for men and women for the treatment of multiple conditions.

did.unittitle: Patent medicine labels
Patent medicine drug labels

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Organized alphabetically by manufacturer name.

did.unittitle: Large patent medicine labels, A-E

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Organized alphabetically by manufacturer name.

did.unittitle: Large patent medicine labels, F-L

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Organized alphabetically by manufacturer name.

did.unittitle: Large patent medicine labels, M-T

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Organized alphabetically by manufacturer name.

did.unittitle: Large patent medicine labels, U-Z

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Organized alphabetically by manufacturer name.

did.unittitle: G. E. Greene labels

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unused product labels printed for G. E. Greene, also known as Greene's Pharmacy located in Hope Valley, Rhode Island. Organized by size and alphabetically by product name.

did.unittitle: Pharmaceutical label companies marketing materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials from Allain-Elbeuf; Drug Package, Incorporated; Senseman Printing Company; and Phoenix Label Company.

did.unittitle: Patent medicine labels and trade cards scrapbooks
Drug labels album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook titled "Drug Labels" with container labels for many different products pasted to pages. Many of the labels are from pharmacies that appear to be related including E. G. Champlin and Company in Westerly, Rhode Island; J. H. Potter, Druggist and Apothecary, Westerly, Rhode Island; Potter and Champlin, Druggists, Westerly, Rhode Island; Greene and Champlin, Wyoming, Rhode Island; and G. E. Greene, with labels listing Wyoming or Hope Valley, Rhode Island as locations. There are also several labels from H. O. Williams, Druggist in Old Mystic, Connecticut, and C. E. Brayton and Company in Stonington, Connecticut and some labels from branded products, likely marketed by wholesalers. The album also includes several receipts and checks from the Stonington Bank for goods and shipment of goods to Stonington, Connecticut dating 1860-1882.

did.unittitle: Jennie Stevens Bristol album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album with pasted in trade cards for a variety of products and businesses in New Haven and other parts of Connecticut. Written on inside of front cover "Made in 1887-1888 by Jennie Stevens Bristol." Product trade cards include patent medicines; foodstuffs, including coffee, tea, baking soda, corned beef, etc.; shoes and boots; clothing and accessories; goods such as soap and washing powder, stove polish, dressmaking patterns, sewing machines, organs, stationary, seeds, and others. Trade cards for patent medicines include Hunt's Remedy for kidney and liver ailments; Pond's Extract Company; Dr. Morse's Compound Syrup of Yellow Dock Root; Colton's Nervine Strengthening Bitters; Schenck's Mandrake Pills "for all bilious complaints"; Horseford's Acid Phosphate "for mental and physical exhaustion, dyspepsia"; Hood's Sarsparilla; Tarrant's Seltzer Aperient; Ayer's Cherry Pectoral for colds and coughs; and Carter's Iron Pills and Little Nerve Pills. Several trade cards in this scrapbook depict Black people using racist stereotypes.

did.unittitle: Pharmacies
Atwood Machine Company order slips

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Order slips for pharmacy supply purchases from Atwood Machine Company, Stonington, Connecticut. Several are signed C. A. Cowan.

did.unittitle: Drug company invoices

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 items, Collins Brothers Drug Company, Meyer Brothers Drug Company, John Wyeth and Brother, C.J. Lincoln Company, Moffit-West Drug Company, Seth H, Mead, M. Augusta Fairchild, Frederick Stearns and Company, Clapp and Townsend

did.unittitle: Druggist vinegar valentine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One page print with insulting poem titled "The Druggist" and full color illustration.

did.unittitle: G. E. Greene, Greene's Pharmacy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph of Greene standing behind the counter of his pharmacy in Hope Valley, Rhode Island, and an envelope circa 1866, the first year the pharmacy was open, when the name was Greene and Champlin.

did.unittitle: H. and J. Brewer, Pharmacists, Springfield, Massachusetts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Small prescription envelope.

did.unittitle: Philips Roxane Laboratories, Your pharmacy coloring book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 page coloring book with illustrations and text about pharmacists and products sold at pharmacies.

did.unittitle: Prescription slips, Sayle's Prescription Pharmacy, New London, Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prescriptions written on Sayle's Prescription Pharmacy prescription pads.

did.unittitle: Prescription slips, multiple pharmacies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prescriptions written on prescription pads from multiple Connecticut pharmacies. Arranged aphabetically by pharmacy name.

did.unittitle: Prescription slips, physicians

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prescriptions written on physicians' personal prescription pads. Arranged alphabetically by physician last name.

did.unittitle: Prescription slips, unmarked paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prescriptions written on unmarked pieces of paper.

did.unittitle: Railroad freight receipts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Receipts for transport of goods to Stonington, Connecticut.

did.unittitle: Rathbone's Ready Reference Guide

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Small pamphlet with train schedules and advertisements for businesses in Connecticut and Rhode Island, including Westerly Drug Company and Mystic Pharmacy.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 items, table of number of cork tops, stationery to A. B. Collins, French ad for a thermometer
